topic.name: Healthcare Policy and Access
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to healthcare services, coverage, and support for various populations, including seniors and individuals facing health challenges.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to remove restrictions for service providers under the Purchased/Referred Care program.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study the impact of this Act on access to care.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make matching grants for cancer screening and referrals.
Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to allow coverage for individuals screened for cancer.
Document 3: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) regarding the payment of income from a qualifying annuity for Medicaid eligibility.
Considers income from an annuity available to the institutionalized spouse.
Document 4: Amends part B of title XVIII (Medicare) to cover qualified acupuncturist services.
Expands healthcare options under Medicare.
Document 5: Prohibits denial of payments for approved educational activities for nursing programs under Medicare.
Supports nursing and allied health education programs.
Document 6: Establishes telehealth pilot projects to analyze clinical outcomes and cost effectiveness.
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to expand telehealth services in specific facilities.
Document 7: Establishes a Community-Based Institutional Special Needs Plan demonstration program for Medicare beneficiaries.
Provides home and community-based care for eligible seniors.
Document 8: Authorizes an option for citizens to buy into Medicare.
Expands access to Medicare for more individuals.
Document 9: Extends temporary increases in rates for ambulance services under Medicare.
Improves access to emergency medical services.
Document 10: Establishes an Interagency Geriatric Mental Health Planning Council for older adults.
Addresses suicide prevention and mental health services for seniors.

topic.name: Education Grants and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at providing grants and support for improving educational services, school infrastructure, and principal training in public schools.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to eligible entities to assist public elementary or secondary schools to function as full-service community schools.
Requires such schools to participate in community-based efforts to coordinate educational, developmental, family, health, and other comprehensive services.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states, or local educational agencies (LEAs) to reduce the size of core curriculum classes in public elementary and secondary schools.
Requires grant funds to be used for constructing new classroom space, hiring additional teachers, and purchasing portable structures.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to consortia composed of a local educational agency and one or more community-based entities.
Requires such schools to provide access to comprehensive services to students, families, and the community.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Education to award renewable five-year matching grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to recruit, support, and train principals for service in high-need schools.
Requires grantees to provide aspiring principals with a pre-service residency focusing on instructional leadership.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Regulations
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts related to the regulation, leasing, and development of oil and gas resources in the Outer Continental Shelf and other federal lands.

Document 1: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a new oil and gas leasing program under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) for a five-year period
Requires the Secretary to include each state-nominated area in the final program unless the impacts of oil and gas development in a particular area cannot be effectively mitigated.
Document 2: Requires holders of leases authorizing exploration and production of oil or natural gas to develop them diligently

Amends the Mineral Leasing Act and the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to require a lessee to pay an annual fee of $4 per acre on the acres covered by a non-producing lease.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management employee to be subject to random testing for the prohibited use of a controlled substance
Directs the Secretary to conduct monthly inspections of each vessel and facility used for Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) oil or gas drilling.
Document 4: Prohibits the Department of the Interior from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico unless they have been renegotiated to require royalty payments
Rentals or royalties received by the United States under covered leases must be deposited in the Treasury and used for federal budget deficit reduction.
Document 5: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to repeal the requirement that the Secretary of the Interior approve plans for geological and geophysical explorations
Repeals the requirement for approval within 30 days after submission.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct an oil and gas competitive leasing program in the National Petroleum Reserve, Alaska

Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the pertinent federal lands.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from authorizing any new lease for exploration or production of oil or natural gas unless the lessee certifies for each existing lease that the lessee has diligently developed the lands
Establishes a civil penalty for noncompliance with this Act.
Document 8: Declares as having no force or effect all federal prohibitions against the leasing of any area of the outer Continental Shelf for exploration, development, and production of oil and natural gas
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to prepare a strategic five-year plan for the sale of oil and natural gas leases.
Document 9: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 10: Amends the Clean Air Act to include hydrogen sulfide in the list of hazardous air pollutants
Repeals the prohibition on aggregating emissions from any oil and gas exploration or production well.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Security
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming immigration policies and enhancing border security in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the enlistment in the armed forces of aliens unlawfully present in the United States.
Adjusts the status of an alien enlisted under such authority to the status of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence.
Document 2: Establishes an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa for a sponsored alien entrepreneur.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to terminate the status of a sponsored entrepreneur if certain conditions are not met.
Document 3: Makes an alien who fails to attest that he or she will not advocate installing a Sharia law system in the United States inadmissible for U.S. entry.
Requires the visa revocation of an alien advocating the installation of a Sharia law system.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to increase the number of full-time Customs and Border Protection officers.
Authorizes appropriations for improvements to existing U.S. ports of entry to improve border security.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program to enhance security.
Revises probationary and termination provisions.
Document 6: Revises provisions regarding the detention and removal of aliens ordered removed.
Begins the removal period on the date the removal order becomes administratively final.
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider a person born in the United States 'subject to the jurisdiction' of the United States for citizenship at birth purposes only under specific conditions.
Document 8: Establishes an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa for a sponsored alien entrepreneur similar to the 2011 Act.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to terminate the status of a sponsored entrepreneur if certain conditions are not met.
Document 9: States that state and local law enforcement personnel have the inherent authority to investigate, apprehend, or transfer to federal custody aliens in the United States.
Provides for the listing of immigration violators in the National Crime Information Center database.
Document 10: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from approving a petition for classification of an alien as a special immigrant religious worker if the alien's country of nationality does not extend reciprocal immigration treatment.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: The documents discuss the legislative requirements for reporting on foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving child welfare systems and protecting children from abuse and trafficking.

Document 1: UAC State Notification Act of 2014 - Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) or the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify the governor of a state within 48 hours before transferring to the state an unaccompanied alien child in the Secretary's custody.
Document 2: Promoting Accountability and Excellence in Child Welfare Act of 2011 - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a child welfare innovation grant program that provides eligible entities with the necessary flexibility and financial incentives to implement comprehensive reforms to existing child welfare programs.

achieve significant results that improve the well-being of all children in the child welfare system; and incorporate higher standards of accountability for state and local agencies and organizations that provide child welfare services.
Document 3: Strengthening the Child Welfare Response to Human Trafficking Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop and publish guidelines to assist state, Indian tribe, and tribal organization child welfare agencies and juvenile and family courts in efforts to serve appropriately youth who are victims of trafficking.

Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act to require a state plan for foster care and adoption assistance to require reasonable efforts be made to: (1) identify as a victim of trafficking and document appropriately in agency records each child who is such a victim.

Document 6: Helping Families in Mental Health Crisis Act revises duties of the Assistant Secretary for Mental Health.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating mortgage practices, protecting consumers in mortgage transactions, and addressing issues related to loan limits and foreclosure processes.

Document 1: Increases the loan limits during calendar 2009 for the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) single family housing mortgage insurance programs and the reverse mortgage program.
Declares that the maximum dollar amount limitation on the principal obligation of such a mortgage shall be considered to be the dollar amount limitation in effect for 2008.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to deem a mortgage broker to have a fiduciary relationship with the consumer.
Requires a mortgage originator to verify the borrower's reasonable ability to pay the principal and interest, real estate taxes, and homeowners insurance fees and premiums.
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to consider approved a mortgagor's written request to a mortgage loan servicer for a short sale if no notification is received within 45 days.
Declares this Act inapplicable to certain residential mortgages entered into before enactment.
Document 4: Prohibits commencement of any foreclosure in connection with certain residential mortgages unless specified prerequisites are met.
Requires the person commencing the foreclosure to notify the mortgagor, in writing, not less than five days before any action is taken.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to deny any construction of the prohibition against residential mortgage loan originator steering incentives as prohibiting a reduction in compensation to an employee mortgage originator.
Allows for a subsequent offer to the consumer that consists only of a reduction in the APR.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury and the GSEs to enter into an agreement modifying the Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement.
Specifies criteria for treating loans made to GSEs and establishes repayment requirements.
Document 7: Amends the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act to require additional mortgage loan data disclosures.
Directs the establishment of a National Home Mortgage and Loan Performance Registry.
Document 8: Requires the establishment of pilot programs to encourage shared appreciation mortgage modifications under the Home Affordable Modification Program.
Mandates that modifications result in positive net present value for investors.
Document 9: Amends the Truth in Lending Act regarding prohibitions against steering incentives in residential mortgage loan origination.
Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed as prohibiting a mortgage originator from compensating another mortgage originator employee based on the principal amount of the loan.
Document 10: Requires Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to establish a voluntary program for eligible borrowers under the Home Affordable Refinance Program.
Mandates that the GSEs pay up to $1,000 of the closing costs associated with refinancing.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on the conservation and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities, including with local, regional, or international programs and partners, to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems.
Authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.
Authorizes the Administrator of NOAA to undertake actions to minimize injury to a coral reef from vessel impacts.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at supporting agricultural practices, rural education, and nutrition assistance programs in the United States.

Document 1: California Milk Marketing Order Act - Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to designate California as a separate federal milk marketing order.
The order covering California shall have the right to reblend and distribute order receipts.
Document 2: Rural Energy Investment Act of 2013 - Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act regarding the biobased markets program.
Provides loan guarantees and grants to agricultural producers and rural small businesses for renewable energy system purchases.
Document 3: Military Family Nutrition Protection Act of 2009 - Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to exclude combat pay from household income.
Requires states to exclude combat pay from family income in making eligibility determinations under the special supplemental nutrition program.
Document 4: Extend Not Cut SNAP Benefits Act - Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act to extend the benefit calculation of 113.6 % of the June 2008 value of the thrifty food plan for SNAP.
Extends the benefits until September 30, 2016.
Document 5: Rural Education Achievement Program Reauthorization Act of 2011 - Amends the Rural Education Initiative to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program.
Raises federal grant limits when funds available to implement the program equal or exceed $100 million.
Document 6: Farm Safety Net Improvement Act of 2007 - Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act to extend and revise the counter-cyclical payment program.
Provides recourse loans for covered commodities through crop year 2012.
Document 7: Wildfire and Drought Relief for Ranchers and Farmers Act of 2012 - Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to use Commodity Credit Corporation funds for supplemental agricultural disaster programs.
Includes the livestock indemnity program and livestock forage disaster program.
Document 8: School Food Modernization Act - Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to issue loan guarantees to local educational agencies to finance the provision of healthy meals.
Prohibits a loan guarantee from covering more than 90% of a loan's principal.
Document 9: Rural Education Achievement Program Reauthorization Act - Amends part B of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program.
Limits eligibility to LEAs whose schools are all designated with a school locale code of rural.
Document 10: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act to eliminate the requirement for forage producers.
Allows crop disaster and livestock assistance without participation in the insurance pilot program.

topic.name: Data Security and Breach Notification
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the requirements for notifying individuals of data breaches.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
resulted in the theft of or unlawful access to the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 2: Requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data.
Requires a covered entity to notify the Secret Service or the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) of a security breach of personal information involving more than 10,000 individuals.
Document 3: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay any U.S. resident of a security breach.

Exempts agencies or business entities from security breach notification requirements if they provide written certification to the Secret Service that providing such notification would impede a criminal investigation.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Prevention
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at preventing tax fraud and identity theft in relation to tax returns and taxpayer information.

to decline to file such IRS-prepared return, without penalty.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Banking Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming financial regulations and improving banking practices in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal banking regulator from terminating or limiting the deposit insurance of a depository institution solely because it either provides or has provided financial services to a marijuana-related legitimate business.
Grants immunity from federal criminal prosecution or investigation to a depository institution providing financial services to a marijuana-related legitimate business.
Document 2: Requires the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to make the value of the U.S. dollar equal to price of gold.
Entitles all entities that depreciate capital assets for tax purposes to 100% expensing of all capital investment for tax purposes in the year that the investment is made.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal banking agency from suggesting, requesting, or ordering a depository institution to terminate a specific customer account.
Prescribes requirements for notice from the federal banking agency to the depository institution about such a customer account restriction or termination.
Document 4: Subjects bank holding companies with total consolidated assets of $250 billion or more to such enhanced supervision and prudential standards.
Expresses the sense of Congress that consolidated asset size is only one of many factors to be considered in determining systemic risk.
Document 5: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to subject credit-default swap trading clearinghouses to regulation by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.
Directs the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to issue final rules to prohibit fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative acts or practices in connection with such swaps.
Document 6: Establishes the Financial Stability Oversight Council to identify risks to U.S. financial stability.

Promote market discipline, by eliminating expectations on the part of shareholders, creditors, and counterparties of such companies that the Government will shield them from losses in the event of failure.
Document 7: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish in the Federal Register proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Board to exempt from certain escrow or impound requirements a loan secured by a first lien on a consumer's principal dwelling.
Document 8: Establishes the Financial Stability Oversight Council to identify risks to the financial stability of the United States.

Document 9: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Document 10: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 with respect to the repayment of Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) assistance.
